rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope and avoid introducing package-qualified names not present in the model.
If a guarantee references implementation-level features or subcomponents from a component type annex, move the binding to the implementation annex or remove the invalid guarantee.
generic_rules: Only reference AADL features, ports, and data types that are explicitly declared in the model's visible scope.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (e.g., ms, Hz) as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly typed and exposed in the model.
When a diagnostic points to an unresolved identifier or invalid helper, trace the root cause and either fix the reference or remove the clause.
AGREE contracts must be scoped to the component type or implementation level, avoiding cross-scope references that are not valid in the current annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in the correct annex type (type vs. implementation).
Use only identifiers that are declared in the same annex or are visible in the AADL model's scope.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties that are not part of the model's visible scope into AGREE contracts.
Do not reference implementation-level subcomponents or ports in a component type annex without moving the binding to the implementation annex.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about empty packages or parsing errors
